summary: The shared renderer work is real and the required commands passed, but the task is not complete in substance because the shipped behavior only renders `flowchart`/`graph` Mermaid roots and falls back for every other valid Mermaid diagram type. The current product/spec docs describe Mermaid fenced blocks as generally rendering, with fallback reserved for invalid Mermaid source, so the shipped implementation does not yet match the documented contract.
satisfied_exit_criteria: The shared note renderer detects fenced `mermaid` blocks in `src/features/notes/markdown.ts`.
Stored markdown remains raw fence text; the renderer only transforms output.
Unit tests cover one successful Mermaid render path and one failed/fallback path.
`npm run test:unit` and `npm run verify` passed in the provided check run.
missing_requirements: `src/features/notes/markdown.ts` hard-gates rendered output to `flowchart` and `graph` only, and sends all other Mermaid roots to fallback (`src/features/notes/markdown.ts#L630`). That is narrower than the documented Mermaid feature contract.

The relevant specs say Mermaid fenced blocks render as diagrams, with fallback for invalid Mermaid source, not for any valid non-flowchart diagram. See `docs/product-specs/markdown-mermaid-diagrams.md#L4`, `docs/product-specs/markdown-mermaid-diagrams.md#L12`, `docs/product-specs/note-authoring.md#L13`, and `docs/product-specs/markdown-editor-workbench.md#L22`.
Because AGENTS done-definition requires the relevant spec/design docs to match shipped behavior, this spec/implementation mismatch blocks promotion even though the tests are green.
human_review_triggers: Valid Mermaid roots such as `sequenceDiagram` currently degrade to fallback despite docs describing Mermaid diagrams as rendered output.
Spec and implementation are out of sync on whether fallback is only for invalid Mermaid or also for unsupported-but-valid Mermaid roots.
